turns.content:
<objective>
Deeply investigate 3 flaw domains in this Hivemind plugin project. Write debug file then produce root cause analysis AND fix plan.

Read the following file first:
- .hivemind/planning/tools-audit-58-9-2026-06-05/03-flaw-elaboration.md
</objective>

<symptoms>
**Domain A: tmux-copilot Permission Gate**
- ORCHESTRATOR_AGENTS hardcoded to only 4 agent names (tmux-copilot.ts:59-64)
- Non-orchestrator agents (hm-executor, hm-planner, gsd-debugger, etc.) cannot use peek/take-over/send-keys
- USER_SESSION cannot call list-panes — can't discover paneIds
- peek requires paneId (%12) but parent agent knows sessionId (ses_xxx)
- Result: copilot features unusable for most real use cases

**Domain B: Child Session Backchannel**
- No way to read running child's interim output (only counts, not content)
- No way to send structured prompt to running child — forward-prompt sends keystrokes to tmux pane, not session.prompt to child
- No child progress discovery beyond opaque counts (toolCallCount, actionCount)
- 60s timeout too short for research tasks — delegation always times out with 0 tool calls

**Domain C: Agent Repetition/Loop**
- No repetition detection — agent sends same message 5+ times
- No loop termination criteria
- Tool output flooding inflates context
</symptoms>

<mode>
goal: find_root_cause_only
symptoms_prefilled: true
</mode>

<files_to_read>
- src/tools/tmux-copilot.ts (full)
- src/coordination/delegation/coordinator.ts (lines 192-290, 678-723)
- src/tools/delegation/delegation-status.ts (lines 1-50, 130-140, 640-680)
- src/tools/session/session-tracker.ts (list-sessions handler)
</files_to_read>

<debug_file>
Create: .hivemind/planning/tools-audit-58-9-2026-06-05/04-debug-root-cause.md
</debug_file>

<output_requirements>
For each domain, document:
1. Root cause with file:line references
2. All code paths affected
3. Preconditions required to trigger
4. Impact propagation (which other features break)
5. Fix strategy (minimal change that eliminates root cause)
6. Risk assessment of fix
</output_requirements>
